name: aion
description: Aion — a durable workflow engine for Gleam, Rust, and the BEAM. Temporal-class durability with the BEAM's native concurrency, supervision, and fault tolerance, running on the beamr VM.

components.description: Pure domain model: Event enum, Payload, identifiers, WorkflowStatus, filters, error taxonomy. The vocabulary every other component speaks. Leaf crate.
The EventStore persistence contract, StoreError, the InMemoryStore reference implementation, and the shared behavioural conformance suite. Leaf crate (depends only on aion-core).
The default durable EventStore over libSQL. Embedded local file plus embedded-replica sync for the distributed path. Runs the aion-store conformance suite.
The .aion package format: manifest, archive read/write, content-hash versioning and module namespacing.

The core engine. Embeds beamr; owns workflow lifecycle, process-per-workflow management, the supervision tree, .aion module loading, durability and replay (durability module), and timers/signals/queries/children/concurrency (time module). Transport-agnostic.
Rust helper crate for writing and registering NIFs that Gleam activities call. Deterministic helpers and recorded light in-VM activities, with the split enforced by type.
The shared wire contract: gRPC service definitions and serde wire types used by the server, all client SDKs, and all worker SDKs. Depends only on aion-core for type parity.
The standalone deployable. Wraps the transport-agnostic engine with HTTP/gRPC APIs, WebSocket event streaming, the remote-worker protocol endpoint, multi-tenancy, and dashboard hosting.
The Rust remote-worker SDK. Registers activity types, receives pushed tasks over the gRPC worker protocol, executes them out-of-process, reports results and heartbeats.
The Rust caller SDK: start, signal, query, cancel, list, subscribe over the aion-proto wire contract.
The Gleam authoring SDK (Hex). workflow, activity, signal, query, timer, child, error, and testing modules, with @external bindings to the engine NIFs. Compiles to the Erlang target, runs on beamr.
The Gleam caller SDK: start, signal, query, cancel, list over the aion-server API.
The Python remote-worker SDK. The home for ML inference and existing Python business logic as out-of-process activities.
The Python caller SDK for starting, signalling, querying, and cancelling workflows.
The TypeScript/Node remote-worker SDK for out-of-process activities in the JS ecosystem.
The TypeScript caller SDK for starting, signalling, querying, and cancelling workflows.
The ops console web UI: workflow list, history viewer, and live event feed over WebSocket. React + Vite + Tailwind.

planned.description: Alternative PostgreSQL backend for teams that prefer it.
Optional server-side Gleam compile/type-check/package via the gleam binary.
Elixir authoring SDK, blocked on beamr opcode/BIF coverage for Elixir.
